players.name: Xeebs
matchupItemFlags: no magic resist vs a 3-magical enemy comp

notes: Their main damage was Countess (32k magical); 1/5 of you itemized magic resist — that hurt.
Enemy ran 1/5 on or near their meta core — they were off-meta too, the win was there.

draftNote: 1 of five queued off a bottom-two lane: Cuban Noobster (midlane→carry).

coaching.headline: Both teams drafted off-meta, so the win was there — you lost it to Countess and bad fight choices.
coaching.team: This one was winnable: the enemy ran only 1 of 5 on their meta core, so the draft edge was actually yours (carry, mid and support all favored). You even won the opening fight at 5.7 (3-2). It slipped on fight selection and one build hole — Countess (32k magical) was their carry and only one of you itemized magic resist, so she kept blowing you up. The decisive loss was the 22.4 fight at their Fangtooth (0-3), which let them roll Genesis Core, Orb Prime and Primal Fangtooth in a row, and the game ended on a 29-min bad trade deep in your own jungle (2-4). Towers: 4 to their 9. With an off-meta enemy, the play was to stop force-fighting Countess and build the one resist item that turns her off.
coaching.whatShiftedIt: Losing the 22.4 fight at their Fangtooth (0-3) gave away Genesis Core, Orb Prime and Primal Fangtooth back-to-back — and only 1 of 5 built magic resist against Countess's 32k.

perPlayer.7c8ab99c-31c5-4ea5-a6fa-e0d6417c3f1b: Murdock was the favored carry and your Vanquisher landed at 25, Imperator at 31 — but you weren't online for the fight that lost it (22.4). 5 deaths from getting caught. As the ranged carry, your damage needs to be present and safe in the team fights; being absent or dead in the decisive one is how a favored lane gets wasted.
perPlayer.16f28434-561f-4f23-9454-40321f4a1ef3: Sevarog — only 1 death, but your hero damage (284/min) was 55% below your norm and you were missing from the key fights. As the offlane and frontline (an even matchup vs Grux), the team needed your body in the 22.4 Fangtooth fight to absorb Countess; instead it was a 0-3. You ran no magic resist into a 3-magical comp too.
perPlayer.a0c393cc-1437-4bdc-8168-64676ce2166c: The Fey was favored but you died 8 times, twice your norm. You were in the 19.6 and 29-min losing fights. As a mid with pickoff power, your deaths came from being too far forward; against an off-meta enemy you should have been farming the lead and picking, not trading into Countess.
perPlayer.10edbf65-457d-48d0-9151-9df5b4fcb40a: Steel was the favored support with 37k mitigated — the frontline was there. But the team had no magic resist as a group against Countess, and you were in the 22.4 and 29-min losses. As the engage, after the early 5.7 win the call was to press the advantage on objectives, not to fight Countess on their Fangtooth.
perPlayer.a412baf3-dd17-4f89-8ecf-9d0704f3a79e: Feng Mao led the team in kills (7) but died 7 times with no magic resist into a 3-magical comp. You were in the 22.4 Fangtooth loss and the 29-min jungle throw. As jungler with the draft edge, the move was to set up safer objectives and not flip the 29.2 bad trade deep in your own jungle, which you started 5v5 with the offlane pinned across the map.

threats: Chain-CC risk (5 hard-CC abilities, ~3.8s of lockdown): Countess Feast (suppress 0.75s), Riktor Riplash (pull), Riktor Shock Therapy (silence 0.8s), Riktor Skewer (suppress 1.25s), Grux Crush (knockup 0.5s). Don't group tight into it — stagger the engage and hold mobility/cleanse for the key one.
Dive threat on your backline: Countess. Assign peel for your carry and ward your flanks.
synergy: Little hard CC on your side — you can't force a pick. Play for poke/objectives and punish their cooldowns, don't flip coin-toss 5v5s.
Strong AoE/teamfight kit — group at choke points and fight on top of objectives.

ourKits.combo: Static Trap root → Buckshot → Hot Pursuit AS → Long Arm of the Law global snipe
ourKits.winCondition: trap for the root, then out-range with Shots Fired and finish with the global ult
ourKits.passive: every 5th basic is a long-range bonus shot
ourKits.playWith: frontline/peel to protect his low mobility
ourKits.playAgainst: almost no escape — engage past the trap and he dies

ourKits.combo: Phantom Rush → Subjugate root → Siphon → Colossal Blow
ourKits.winCondition: stack Souls to snowball Siphon and lock down with the root
ourKits.passive: ability kills grant permanent Soul stacks (HP + damage)
ourKits.playWith: a team that converts his root into kills
ourKits.playAgainst: deny his last-hits early to starve Souls; dodge the slow Subjugate

ourKits.combo: Bramble Patch slow → Harvest Nettles stacks → Untamed Growth DoT → Fly Trap pull ult
ourKits.winCondition: Fly Trap a clumped team and detonate AoE on the pull
ourKits.passive: takedowns spawn growths + grant move speed
ourKits.playWith: engage that clumps for her Fly Trap
ourKits.playAgainst: spread out vs the pull; she's squishy — dive her

ourKits.combo: Bull Rush knockback → Force Shield wall → Shield Slam knockup+stun ult, Shield Bash %hp
ourKits.winCondition: peel the carry with walls/knockups or engage with Shield Slam
ourKits.playWith: a carry he can wall for and peel
ourKits.playAgainst: his Force Shield blocks projectiles — reposition around it; bait Shield Slam

ourKits.combo: Reaping Dash → Hamstring slows → Safeguard spellshield → Earth Shatter execute
ourKits.winCondition: trade with the spellshield up and execute low targets with Earth Shatter
ourKits.passive: permanent power on unique hero takedowns
ourKits.playWith: setup that holds a target for the execute
ourKits.playAgainst: bait Safeguard's spellshield before committing your key CC

enemyKits.combo: Boreal Sweep slow → Hoarfrost root → Glacial Charge engage → Cryoseism stun ult
enemyKits.winCondition: chain frost CC (slow→root→stun) to lock the fight down
enemyKits.playWith: burst that converts her long CC chain into kills
enemyKits.playAgainst: she's a CC battery — cleanse/QSS the Cryoseism stun; spread to avoid the chain

enemyKits.combo: Shadow Slip in → Eventide → Blade Siphon → Feast suppress ult, Haemorrhage true-damage
enemyKits.winCondition: Feast-suppress a squishy and delete them with true-damage burst
enemyKits.passive: two-ability hits apply a %max-HP true-damage bleed
enemyKits.playWith: a team to clean up after her pick
enemyKits.playAgainst: Feast is her whole engage — bait it / peel; she's fragile, punish the recast window

enemyKits.combo: Riplash pull → Shock Therapy silence → Skewer suppress ult
enemyKits.winCondition: pull a target in and chain silence+suppress for a guaranteed kill
enemyKits.playWith: burst that deletes his pulled/suppressed target
enemyKits.playAgainst: respect Riplash range — stay spaced; cleanse/QSS the suppress

enemyKits.combo: Piercing Shot poke → Heightened Senses AS → Hail of Arrows zone → Inner Fire ult
enemyKits.winCondition: ramp Relentless + AS and sustained-DPS the front down
enemyKits.playWith: heavy peel — she's immobile and pure DPS
enemyKits.playAgainst: she has zero escape — hard-engage her and she dies first

enemyKits.combo: Smash 'n Grab pull → Crush knockup → Warlord's Challenge stun → bleed-stacked basics
enemyKits.winCondition: land the pull→knockup chain to lock a target while bleed and AS shred them
enemyKits.passive: basics stack a bleed for sustained damage
enemyKits.playWith: follow-up burst inside his knockup/stun window
enemyKits.playAgainst: telegraphed engage — bait Crush/Pull, then punish; kite his slow start
